query: Colombia presidential election 2026 political violence June campaign killing attack
answer:
The research agent returned comprehensive findings. Let me compile this into a final structured report.

---

## Colombia 2026 Presidential Election: Political Violence Report

### The Assassination of Miguel Uribe Turbay — The Defining Event

The most significant act of political violence in Colombia's 2026 election cycle was the **shooting and subsequent death of Senator Miguel Uribe Turbay**, a Democratic Centre pre-candidate for the presidency.

**The Attack (June 7, 2025):**
- Uribe Turbay was **shot from behind during a campaign rally** at El Golfito Park in the Modelia neighborhood of Bogotá at approximately 5:00 PM.
- He was hit **three times, including two shots to the head**, with a 9mm Glock-style pistol traced to a legal purchase in Mesa, Arizona, USA.
- Two other people were injured; the suspected gunman (a 15-year-old boy acting as a paid hitman) was wounded during exchange of fire with bodyguards.

**Death (August 11, 2025):**
- After multiple surgeries and a prolonged hospitalization at Fundación Santa Fe de Bogotá, Uribe Turbay **died on August 11, 2025** from complications of his injuries — two months after the shooting.

**The Victim's Background:**
- Age 39 at death; grandson of former President Julio César Turbay Ayala
- Son of journalist **Diana Turbay**, who was kidnapped by the Medellín Cartel in 1990 and killed during a botched rescue in 1991
- His family had thus suffered political violence across two generations

**Investigation:**
- **Six people arrested**, including the 15-year-old shooter (sentenced to 7 years in a youth rehabilitation facility on August 27, 2025) and alleged mastermind Elder José Arteaga Hernández ("Chipi"), arrested July 5, 2025.
- The teenage shooter said at arrest: *"I'm sorry, I did it for the money, for my family."*
- As of last reports, **no definitive motive had been publicly established**.
- The Ministry of National Defence offered **3 billion Colombian pesos** for information.
- President Petro raised concerns that Uribe Turbay's **security detail had been reduced** shortly before the attack and ordered an investigation.

**Aftermath:**
- Uribe Turbay's father, **Miguel Uribe Londoño**, launched his own presidential campaign on August 26, 2025, to "continue his son's legacy," though he ultimately received only ~28,657 votes (<1%) in the May 31, 2026 first round.
- France 24 described the attack as one that **"revived the darkest hours of political violence in Colombia."**

---

### The 2026 Election: Current Status

**First Round (May 31, 2026):** Turnout was 57.9% (~23.7 million valid votes)

| Candidate | Coalition | Votes | % |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Abelardo de la Espriella** | Independent/National Salvation Movement | 10,361,499 | 43.7% |
| **Iván Cepeda** | Historic Pact/Alliance for Life | 9,688,361 | 40.9% |
| Paloma Valencia | Democratic Centre | 1,639,685 | 6.9% |
| Sergio Fajardo | Dignity and Commitment | 1,009,073 | ~4.3% |

**Runoff:** De la Espriella vs. Cepeda scheduled for **June 21, 2026**.

---

### Security Context for Runoff Candidates

**Iván Cepeda (left-wing):** His father, Senator Manuel Cepeda Vargas, was **assassinated on August 9, 1994** by state agents and paramilitaries. Cepeda himself has faced repeated death threats throughout his career and was forced into exile in France in 2000. He is a leading advocate for victims of the **Patriotic Union genocide** (5,733 murdered militants by official count).

**Abelardo de la Espriella (right-wing):** Has historical ties to paramilitary-linked figures including **Salvatore Mancuso** (AUC commander). His FIPAZ Foundation was found by Colombia's Supreme Court to have served as a vehicle for paramilitary political projection.

---

### Historical Context: Colombia's Pattern of Election Violence

Colombia has suffered some of the worst electoral violence in the Western Hemisphere:
- **Patriotic Union genocide (1985–present):** 5,733 murdered militants, including two presidential candidates (Jaime Pardo Leal, 1987; Bernardo Jaramillo Ossa, 1990)
- **Luis Carlos Galán** (1989): Liberal Party presidential frontrunner, assassinated by the Medellín Cartel
- **Carlos Pizarro Leongómez** (1990): M-19 presidential candidate, assassinated on a commercial flight
- The ongoing armed conflict involves ELN guerrillas, drug cartels, and paramilitary successor groups; President Petro's "Total Peace" policy to demobilize remaining armed groups is "generally regarded as a failure"

---

### International Reactions to Uribe Turbay Assassination
- **OAS:** Condemned the violence, called for investigation
- **UN Secretary-General:** Called for full investigation
- **Human Rights Watch:** Said the attack "risked deterring political participation"
- **US Secretary of State Marco Rubio:** Condemned the attack
- Multiple regional leaders (Chile, Ecuador, Venezuela opposition) condemned the shooting

---

### Data Limitations
This research could not access:
- **INDEPAZ** statistics on social leader and candidate killings during the full 2026 cycle
- **MOE (Misión de Observación Electoral)** violence monitoring reports
- Incidents involving lower-profile candidates, campaign workers, or local officials
- Detailed security arrangements for the June 21, 2026 runoff

key_facts: Senator Miguel Uribe Turbay, a Democratic Centre pre-candidate for the 2026 Colombian presidency, was shot three times (including twice in the head) at a campaign rally in Bogotá on June 7, 2025, and died on August 11, 2025.
The shooter was a 15-year-old hired hitman who said at arrest: 'I'm sorry, I did it for the money, for my family.' He was sentenced to 7 years in a youth rehabilitation facility on August 27, 2025.

Six people have been arrested in connection with the assassination; the alleged mastermind Elder José Arteaga Hernández was arrested on July 5, 2025. No definitive motive has been publicly established.
The weapon was a 9mm Glock-style pistol traced to a legal purchase on August 6, 2020, in Mesa, Arizona, USA — highlighting transnational arms trafficking.
President Petro raised concerns that Uribe Turbay's security detail had been reduced shortly before the shooting and ordered an investigation.
The Colombian Ministry of National Defence offered a reward of up to 3 billion Colombian pesos for information about the attack.

Uribe Turbay was the grandson of former President Julio César Turbay Ayala and son of journalist Diana Turbay, who was killed by the Medellín Cartel in 1991 — making this a multi-generational political violence case.
The 2026 first round (May 31) produced a runoff between right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella (43.7%, 10.36M votes) and left-wing Iván Cepeda (40.9%, 9.69M votes), scheduled for June 21, 2026.
First round turnout was 57.9%, an increase of 2.92 percentage points over the previous election.

Iván Cepeda's father, Senator Manuel Cepeda Vargas, was assassinated on August 9, 1994, by state agents and paramilitaries — both runoff candidates have connections to Colombia's violent political history.
The Patriotic Union genocide resulted in 5,733 murdered militants according to Colombian justice system counts, including two presidential candidates.
France 24 described the Uribe Turbay attack as one that 'revived the darkest hours of political violence in Colombia.'
Human Rights Watch said the attack 'risked deterring political participation' and called for prompt, transparent investigations.

On May 29, 2026, Ecuadorean President Daniel Noboa met with candidate de la Espriella and announced elimination of a security tax, which the Colombian government called 'deliberate interference' in the election.
